title: Add Time Controls below Global Control button (surgical placement only)

requirements.summary: Insert TimeControls component directly below the Global Control button in the left panel of LocationMapExplorer.tsx, without changing any other layout elements, styling, or component positions from v1153
acceptanceCriteria: Time Controls section appears directly below the Global Control button in the left panel
Global Control button remains below the Day/Night Terminator and Twilight Zone controls, as in v1153
No other panel sections, buttons, or layout elements have moved, been restyled, or been removed compared to v1153
All other UI elements (map, right panel, top bar, music player, etc.) are pixel-identical to v1153

file_operations.description: In the left panel JSX structure, locate the Global Control button section (which sits below the Day/Night Terminator and Twilight Zone controls). Immediately after the closing tag of the Global Control button's parent container, insert the TimeControls component with the exact same props and styling it currently has elsewhere in the file. Remove the TimeControls component from its old location (if it exists elsewhere in the left panel). This is a surgical DOM reordering only — do not modify any other sections, remove any other elements, change class names, or alter styling of any existing controls. Verify the left panel structure matches v1153 except for the TimeControls insertion point.

requirements.summary: Ensure the TimeControls component retains its full original functionality including Real, Hourly, and Yearly modes with play/pause controls that animate the solar terminator on the InteractiveGlobe
acceptanceCriteria: Real, Hourly, and Yearly mode buttons are present and functional in the TimeControls component
Play/pause control animates the terminator across the 3D globe when activated
The solar terminator position on the InteractiveGlobe visually updates as time progresses during animation
Stopping or pausing the animation freezes the terminator at the current time position

file_operations.description: Review the state variables and callbacks that drive the TimeControls component (timeMode, isPlaying, currentTime, handleTimeChange, handleTimeModeChange, handlePlayPause). Ensure all these state variables are correctly wired to the TimeControls component via props after the DOM move. Verify that the InteractiveGlobe component receives the currentTime prop so the terminator position updates during animation. If any state wiring is missing or broken after the layout change, restore it to match the working implementation. Do not modify the TimeControls component itself or the InteractiveGlobe's terminator rendering logic.

Review the TimeControls component to confirm it correctly receives and uses all required props (timeMode, isPlaying, currentTime, onTimeChange, onTimeModeChange, onPlayPause). If any prop handling is missing or the component is purely presentational without callbacks, ensure the component dispatches the user's interactions (mode selection, play/pause clicks) back to the parent via the provided callback props. Do not change the component's visual appearance or add new features; only ensure proper event handling and prop usage for full functionality.

Verify that the InteractiveGlobe component accepts a currentTime prop (or similar time state) and uses it to compute and render the solar terminator position on the 3D globe. If the terminator rendering logic exists but is not connected to a time prop, add the prop and wire it into the terminator calculation. Reference the SunCalc library (loaded via CDN in index.html) or the solarCalculations utility for computing sun position based on currentTime. Do not modify the globe's visual style, camera controls, or any other rendering features beyond the terminator position update.
